title: Battle at Primus Goluud
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Battle |prev=[[Battle of Kirrek (Great Hyperspace War)|Battle of Kirrek]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] |conc= |next=[[First battle of Korriban]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] |image=[[File:Battle_of_Primus_Goluud.jpg]] |name=Battle at Primus Goluud |conflict=[[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] |date=[[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |place=[[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]] |result=Victory for [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros]] forces |side1=[[Koros fleet]] |side2=[[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] |commanders2=[[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*[[Koros Spaceworks interceptor]]s *[[Koros starfighter|Koros Spaceworks Starfighters]] *[[Unidentified Koros system battleship|Sienar Battleship]] |forces2=*[[Sith starfighter]]s *[[Derriphan-class battleship|''Derriphan''-class battleships]] **[[Corsair (Derriphan-class)|''Corsair'']] *[[Naga Sadow's Mothership]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=[[Gav Daragon]] |casual2=None |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|It's a trap!''"
"''…No, it's an opportunity!|Members of the Koros force, after sighting the Sith fleet|}} The '''Battle at Primus Goluud''' was waged high above the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]] during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], between [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta's]] [[Koros fleet|forces]] and the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]. During the earlier [[Battle of Koros Major (Great Hyperspace War)|Battle of Koros Major]], [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Gav Daragon]] was confronted by his sister, [[Jori Daragon]], over the [[Death/Legends|death]] of their acquaintance [[Aarrba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. Overcome by emotion, Gav left and traveled to the [[Primus Goluud system]], where Sadow was sequestered in his [[Naga Sadow's Mothership|Sith Meditation Sphere]] creating [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]] to aid the Sith war effort. Daragon, wanting to end the violence of the war, fired on the [[Sith Meditation Sphere|Meditation Sphere]], damaging it. However, Sadow requested Daragon to go to the damaged sphere to face him man-to-man, an offer Daragon accepted—only to find he had been tricked and Sadow had trapped him there. Daragon frantically transmitted vital strategic information to Teta's forces from the sphere, hoping to aid in a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] victory in the war; the Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] quickly arrived. However, Sadow's forces reappeared in the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|system]] soon afterward, led by Sadow's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Corsair (Derriphan-class)|''Corsair'']]. [[Laser/Legends|Laser]] fire erupted between the two sides, although Sadow had a trick up his sleeve; Sadow used a [[Supernova (Sith magic)|secret weapon]] to trigger Primus Goluud's solar flares, destroying the sphere and killing Daragon, though not before the Koros forces were able to escape. ==Prelude== {{Quote|Maybe that'll break his concentration!|Gav Daragon, after firing on Naga Sadow's meditation sphere|}} In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], the expansionist ruler of the long-isolated [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], gathered an invasion force and attacked the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] strategy in the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], as it came to be called, was to launch a two-pronged attack—one part of the Sith force [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|attacked]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the Republic, while the other part attacked the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]], with [[Battle of Kirrek (Great Hyperspace War)|two]] [[Battle of Koros Major (Great Hyperspace War)|battles]] occurring on the [[Planet/Legends|planets]] [[Kirrek]] and [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]]. While all this happened, Sadow retreated in his [[Naga Sadow's Mothership|Sith Meditation Sphere]] to the remote [[Primus Goluud system]], where he was deep in concentration supplementing the Sith forces with his [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]]. Meanwhile, on Koros Major, [[Gav Daragon]], [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the Sith effort on the planet, took a [[Dropship/Legends|dropship]] down to the planet to try and find his sister, [[Jori Daragon]], amidst the battle. During his search, Daragon got into an altercation with an old acquaintance, [[Aarrba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], resulting in the Hutt's [[Death/Legends|death]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]] Jori Daragon arrived on the scene at that moment, and confronted her brother about Aarrba's death; overcome by emotion, Gav fled. [[File:Battle_at_Primus_Goluud_Prelude-Timeline12.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Gav Daragon's battleship fires on Sadow's Naga Sadow's Mothership]] Gav decided to go to the Primus Goluud [[Star system/Legends|system]] and confront Sadow, hoping to end the war in one swift stroke. Daragon [[Laser/Legends|fired]] on Sadow's [[Sith Meditation Sphere]], breaking his concentration and causing the disappearance of his illusions. Sadow then sent Daragon a message, asking him to come to the Meditation Sphere and face him man-to-man. Daragon accepted, and took his personal [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] across to the sphere. However, when Daragon arrived on the sphere, he found it empty; Sadow had deserted it and stranded him there, in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the system's [[Color/Legends|red]] supergiant [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]]. Frantic, Daragon used the sphere's still-working [[Comm unit/Legends|comm system]] to contact his sister and [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]], the ruler of the Koros system. Their [[Koros fleet|forces]] quickly arrived, instructing Daragon to surrender and prepare to be boarded; in response, Daragon renounced the Sith Empire and told them that the Empire was unguarded; information that could help them seal a victory against the Sith. ==The battle== {{Quote|A Dark Lord of the Sith does not give up so easily!|Naga Sadow|}} [[File:Battle_of_Primus_Goluud-Timeline12.jpg|thumb|250px|Battle of Primus Goluud]] However, at that moment, the remnants of Sadow's battered [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] were recalled, gathering in the Primus Goluud system. Sadow boasted that a Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] of the Sith would not give up so easily; although the Koros forces were at first surprised, they saw it as an opportunity to do a great deal of damage to the Sith force. Laser fire ensued between the two sides, and Sadow, aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Derriphan-class battleship|''Derriphan''-class battleship]] [[Corsair (Derriphan-class)|''Corsair'']], used a [[Supernova (Sith magic)|secret weapon]] to trigger the unstable red star's solar flares. With the flares threatening to kill them all, Daragon warned his sister and the rest of the Koros forces to flee, and transmitted the coordinates of the Sith Empire to them. The Sith left to return to their homes, and Empress Teta gave the order to follow Daragon's coordinates back to the Sith Empire. The Sith Meditation Sphere containing Gav Daragon was destroyed, killing him. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Goodbye, Jori.|Gav Daragon's last words|}} With Daragon's coordinates in hand, Teta's forces followed Sadow back to the Sith Empire and handed him a final defeat at the [[second battle of Korriban]]. Sadow was forced to fake his death and flee to the faraway [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. After Gav Daragon's death in the Battle of Primus Goluud, Jori Daragon took over operations of [[Aarrba's Repair Dock]], the business that the late Aarrba the Hutt had owned in [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]], the capital [[City/Legends|city]] of Koros Major, in Gav's memory. ==Behind the scenes== The Battle at Primus Goluud was an event occurring in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1996]]. The battle took place over several pages of the arc's fourth issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''The Dogs of War'']]. A galactic history entry in the in-game Codex of the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' was the first to provide a name for the battle.{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 28: The Battle at Primus Goluud}} ==Appearances== *''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Precipice]]'' {{Mo}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] {{Mo}} *''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Savior]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} {{1stID|Battle at Primus Goluud}} *''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Path of Destruction (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] {{Ab|Darth Plagueis (audiobook)}} {{Imo}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{Imo}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] {{Imo}} *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{1stID|Battle of Primus Goluud}} *{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 12: The Great Hyperspace War|int=Timeline 12: The Great Hyperspace War}} {{Hologram}} *[[The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural (real-life book)|''The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural'']] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GreatHyperspaceWarNav}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Primus Goluud |es=Batalla en Primus Goluud |it=Battaglia di Primus Goluud |pl=Bitwa o Primus Goluud }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Primus Goluud, Battle at}} [[Category:Artificial disasters]] [[Category:Battles in the Deep Core]] [[Category:Battles of the Great Hyperspace War]] [[Category:Supernovas]]
